expected_curve: value_profile flat-high; value_profile_cmplog monotone decreasing in ARM_COUNT (gap grows with K)

acceptance.rule: value_profile ≈ value_profile_cmplog at ARM_COUNT=2; value_profile > value_profile_cmplog at ARM_COUNT=8; value_profile >> value_profile_cmplog (vpc near 0) at ARM_COUNT=16

design_basis: Both fuzzers share the per-arm CMP_MAP gradient that climbs the 4-byte selector to the target constant, so value_profile resolves at every K. value_profile_cmplog additionally logs all K case constants into its I2S dictionary; each per-mutation I2S draw substitutes a sister-arm constant into the selector with ~(K-1)/K probability, monoculturing the corpus into a non-target arm. The target-hit probability per I2S draw is ~1/K, so the roulette overhead — and the gap to value_profile — grows with ARM_COUNT.
notes: ARM_COUNT=2: I2S draw hits the target ~1/2 of the time; vpc's roulette barely competes with the gradient, so vp ≈ vpc.
ARM_COUNT=4: ~1/4 target draws; vpc starts lagging as sister arms gain corpus residency.
ARM_COUNT=8: ~1/8 target draws; vpc clearly slower, vp still flat-high.
ARM_COUNT=16: ~1/16 target draws; vpc's dictionary roulette dominates the gradient, vpc expected near 0, vp still resolves.

HONESTY: the monoculture/scheduler-residency half of the mechanism is emergent and may not reproduce in a single 4-byte-selector micro-harness; a flat (no-gap) result is an honest 'inconclusive', not a forced failure.
